title: Questions to Ask a Solar Installer
description: Key questions to ask before committing to a solar installation.
keyTakeaways: Ask for total installed cost per watt

sections.title: Pricing Questions
sections.content: Ask for total installed cost, cash price, financed price, dealer fees, and what is excluded.

sections.title: Equipment Questions
sections.content: Ask which panels, inverters, batteries, monitoring, and mounting systems are included.

sections.title: Contract Questions
sections.content: Ask about timelines, cancellation, roof work, production guarantees, and warranty service.

sections.title: Questions About Production Estimates
sections.content: Ask which shade model, weather data, degradation assumption, and utility rate plan the estimate uses. A credible proposal should explain why the projected kWh output is realistic for your roof.

sections.title: Questions About Contract Risk
sections.content: Ask who handles permits, interconnection, utility approvals, roof penetrations, workmanship warranty claims, and equipment replacements if the original installer is unavailable.

faqs.question: How many quotes should I get?
faqs.answer: At least three quotes is a good starting point for comparing pricing and scope.

faqs.question: Should I get more than one solar quote?
faqs.answer: Yes. Comparing at least two or three quotes helps reveal pricing differences, equipment tradeoffs, and financing assumptions that may not be obvious in a single proposal.

sources: U.S. Department of Energy solar homeowner resources
IRS residential clean energy credit guidance
DSIRE state and utility incentive database
